human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for public-service delivery boundary discipline, source quality, official-record and privacy prerequisite handling, eligibility and due-process escalation logic, public-obligation handling, procurement and public-records handoff posture, equity and language-access escalation, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to public-service delivery support, official-record review, documented service-rule application, stakeholder communication, and escalation packaging grounded in program, eligibility, notice, records, procurement, equity, and audit artifacts.

Authority sources cover OMB service-delivery policy, OMB digital-first public experience guidance, 21st Century IDEA, FOIA, Privacy Act, NARA records requirements, procurement operating rules, APA due-process guardrails, GAO internal-control standards, and DOJ Title VI civil-rights guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope status resolution, missing facts, unsupported policy exceptions, eligibility and rights-sensitive escalation, missing audit trails, adjudication and enforcement refusal, legal disclosure conflicts, procurement handoff, repeat failure, terminology fidelity, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/government-and-public-sector/public-service-delivery-specialist/ so public-service delivery work stays separate from program adjudication, legal, public records, procurement, audit, privacy, enforcement, and civil-rights ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around eligibility decisions, appeal and notice handling, public-records disclosure, privacy release, procurement-sensitive communication, enforcement actions, public-obligation claims, equity-sensitive service access, and legal interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated government-and-public-sector public service delivery specialist that provides evidence-backed resolution and disciplined escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained-implementation runtime exists.
